task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal output (e.g., using cat, less) or a text editor showing the contents of ~/Desktop/orbit_raise_specs.txt.

task_completion.description: Agent creates a GMAT script with the required thruster and orbit parameters.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or GMAT GUI showing script code that includes a ChemicalThruster with 400 N thrust, 316 s Isp, and 80 kg fuel mass, as well as orbit parameters for 400 km and 800 km altitudes.

task_completion.description: Agent saves the GMAT script to the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal command (e.g., ls, cp, mv) or file manager showing gravity_loss_mission.script located in the ~/GMAT_output/ directory.

task_completion.description: Agent saves the report text file to the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal command or file manager showing gravity_loss_report.txt located in the ~/GMAT_output/ directory.

task_completion.description: The report contains the required lines with calculated numerical values.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal output or text editor showing the contents of gravity_loss_report.txt containing all 10 required keys (e.g., impulsive_dv1_m_s, gravity_loss_m_s) with actual numerical values instead of the <value> placeholder.

integrity.description: Agent actually runs GMAT to compute the results.
integrity.visual_evidence: The GMAT GUI is open and running the mission, or a terminal shows a command executing GMAT (e.g., GMAT-R2022a or similar) with the created script.

integrity.description: The agent does not hardcode fake results into the report.
integrity.visual_evidence: The numerical values in the report appear to be precise floating-point numbers derived from a simulation, not simple integers, all zeros, or the literal string '<value>'.

integrity.description: The GMAT script contains genuine GMAT syntax.
integrity.visual_evidence: The script file contains valid GMAT objects and commands (e.g., Create Spacecraft, Create ChemicalThruster, Propagate, Target, Maneuver) rather than pseudocode or plain English.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The initial SMA should be approximately 6771 km (400 km altitude) and final SMA 7171 km (800 km altitude). The impulsive total Delta-V should be between 190 and 250 m/s (theoretically ~217 m/s). The finite total Delta-V should be between 195 and 280 m/s. The gravity loss should be a positive value between 1.0 and 35.0 m/s.
